_comment: P29 integration evidence: the shipped tools/videoedit modules exercised live against the P26 spike goldens. Companion to docs/video-tooling-spike-evidence.json (P26, frozen). All media and installs lived in a throwaway scratchpad; only the raw stderr fixtures under tools/videoedit/fixtures/ entered the repo.

ffmpeg.note: identical archive hash to the P26 spike environment

fixture_media.file: workshop-synth.mp4 (scratchpad only, regenerated from the recipe in docs/video-tooling-spike-evidence.json)
fixture_media.note: hash differs from the P26 encode (encoder nondeterminism); ground truth timings identical: cuts 60/150/240/330, silences 92 to 104.5 / 210 to 218.5 / 300 to 320, duration 420.0

live_checks.expected: P26 S-3 golden: cuts [60.0, 150.0, 240.0, 330.0], zero false positives, threshold 27.0
live_checks.observed: cuts [60.0, 150.0, 240.0, 330.0]; computed_by pyscenedetect.ContentDetector; scenedetect 0.7 renamed get_seconds() to the seconds property (handled with a compatibility shim; open validation item 4 resolved)

live_checks.expected: P26 S-1 golden: cuts 150/240/330 (scores 15.6 / 66.0 / 40.6), 60s isoluminant cut absent
live_checks.observed: cuts [150.0, 240.0, 330.0] (scores 15.625 / 66.016 / 40.625); 60s cut absent; luma caveat note attached to the result

live_checks.command: reframe.render(media, out, 100.0, 120.0, crop_geometry(1280,720).crop, backend=moviepy) with shorts_reframe enabled
live_checks.expected: P26 S-5 shape: 9:16 crop of a 1280x720 source, h264 + aac, 20.0s (even-rounded 404x720 vs the spike's odd 405)
live_checks.observed: ffprobe: h264 404x720 + aac, duration 20.000000; renderer moviepy.crop

live_checks.command: initialize + tools/list against tools/mcp_server.py (venv has mcp 1.28.1)
live_checks.observed: 27 tools; silence_scan, scene_scan, reframe_shorts, edit_build_mlt all present

blocked_or_unverified.detail: pip install auto-editor still fails at its post-install binary bootstrap: HTTP 403 from github.com/WyattBlue/auto-editor releases through the container proxy (same as P26 S-2). Open validation item 1 (auto-editor FCPXML through fcpxml.parse) remains unvalidated; auto-editor stays conditionally shortlisted, not integrated.

otio-kdenlive-adapter 0.0.3 cannot read the MLT XML mltxml.py emits (AttributeError on a missing kdenlive-specific element): the adapter expects Kdenlive's own project structure (kdenlive:docproperties), not generic/Shotcut-flavored MLT. Our .mlt targets Shotcut natively; Kdenlive opens generic MLT through its own importer, which is not verifiable in this container. Open validation item 2 partially resolved: adapter interop is a no, editor-side import remains unverified.

No melt binary available in the container; the melt path of mltxml.render is implemented and gate-tested (selftest covers both flag permutations and the honest missing-backend refusal) but has never executed a real render. macOS headless melt (open validation item 3) likewise unverified.
No Shotcut/Kdenlive/FCP available; emitted .mlt files are verified well-formed and round-trip through mltxml.parse only.
